requirements.title: Rule-backed canvas interaction
requirements.description: Replace the right-sidebar scenario page with a large stepwise canvas where scenario and command choices generate nodes and branches.

requirements.title: Local recommendation rule table
requirements.description: Generate or curate a project-local rule table from maestro-flow sources for UI consumption.

requirements.title: Browser-persisted route history and favorites
requirements.description: Persist recent generated route maps and uncapped favorite routes in browser storage.

constraints.constraint: The next implementation MUST be treated as a product-model redesign, not a cosmetic layout-only refactor.
constraints.rationale: The current right-sidebar layout conflicts with the requested canvas interaction.

constraints.constraint: Canvas recommendation rules MUST start from maestro-next lifecycle, scoring, precondition, routing-table, and workflow-cluster concepts.
constraints.rationale: maestro-next already defines the closest recommendation algorithm.

constraints.constraint: Canvas start-node scenario taxonomy SHOULD reuse maestro-help catalog/workflow data.
constraints.rationale: maestro-help contains command categories, task patterns, workflow topology, and Path A-F definitions.

constraints.constraint: Saved routes MUST store recommendation evidence and SHOULD NOT require exact canvas coordinates for MVP.
constraints.rationale: Users need to understand why recommendations were made without binding saved data to a UI layout version.

constraints.constraint: MVP recommendation rules MUST run against browser-side simulated state, not real .workflow/state.json.
constraints.rationale: The static browser UI cannot read local project files without a service or import flow.

constraints.constraint: Recommendation rules MUST cite maestro-flow submodule paths as provenance.
constraints.rationale: The submodule is the true and updateable source of Maestro command behavior.

constraints.constraint: Canvas state MUST support multiple independently growing branches with an active branch selection.
constraints.rationale: The requested interaction includes stepwise expansion and multi-branch operation.

domain.problem_statement: The current Maestro Workflow Wiki page does not match the desired canvas-based command recommendation interaction. The next version should use a large node canvas driven by source-backed local rules from maestro-flow.

terminology.definition: Main interaction surface where scenario and command nodes are arranged visually.
First central node that classifies the user's task scenario before command recommendation.
Maestro-derived category such as project initialization, new requirement, source exploration, or test expansion.
A generated canvas path composed of selected scenario and command nodes.
A user-protected saved route map that is never overwritten by recent-history cleanup.

domain.audience: Users learning which Maestro command or workflow path to choose

non_goals.title: Real project state parsing in MVP
non_goals.rationale: MVP uses simulated browser-side state; real state import/service is future work.

non_goals.title: LLM-backed runtime recommendation
non_goals.rationale: Rules are local and explainable; LLM assists rule updates via protocol, not browser-time inference.

insights.summary: The current page hardcodes a large right-side detail panel that conflicts with the desired canvas model.
maestro-help supplies catalog and workflow taxonomy while maestro-next supplies scoring and next-step recommendation concepts.

open_questions.question: Should parallel branches be displayed as a tree, swimlanes, or auto-layout graph?
What exact TypeScript schema names and fields should be used for command facts, recommendation rules, and saved route payloads?
